title: lathyrus latifolius subsp. heterophyllus
intro: Lathyrus latifolius subsp. heterophyllus, also known as the broad-leaved peacock's ear or hairy peacock lily, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Lathyrus latifolius subsp. heterophyllus include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a valuable plant for traditional medicine. The health benefits associated with consuming Lathyrus latifolius subsp. heterophyllus include improved cardiovascular health, enhanced immune function, and reduced inflammation, although it is essential to note that excessive consumption can be toxic. In traditional herbal preparations, Lathyrus latifolius subsp. heterophyllus is often used as a decoction or infused as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
